description: You use the services in the assets folder to get the checksum of one ore more assets on Integration Server.

services.name: pub.assets:getAssetInfo
description: WmPublic. Retrieves information for a specific asset in a package.

input_parameters.name: packageName
description: Name of the package for which to retrieve assets.

input_parameters.name: assetName
description: Fully qualified name of the asset from which to retrieve asset information.

output_parameters.name: asset
description: The list of information about the specified asset.

children.name: name
description: Fully qualified name of the asset.

children.name: displayName
description: Local name of the asset.

children.name: category
description: Asset category, such as Service, Document, and Trigger.

children.name: type
description: Asset type, such as Flow Service, Java Service, and Document Type.

children.name: dependents
description: A list of assets that depend on or use this asset. If there are no dependents or there are errors retrieving dependents, the service returns an empty list.

children.name: references
description: A list of assets that are referenced or used by this asset. If there are no references or there are any errors retrieving references, the service returns an empty list.

children.name: dependentsError
description: Conditional. Message indicating the error preventing retrieval of a list of dependent assets. This parameter is returned only if there is an error in retrieving the dependent assets.

children.name: referencesError
description: Conditional. Message indicating the error preventing to retrieve a list of referenced assets. This parameter is returned only if there is an error in retrieving the referenced assets.

services.name: pub.assets:getAssetsFromDependentPackages
description: WmPublic. Retrieves asset information for the specified package and any dependent packages.

input_parameters.name: packageName
description: Name of the package from which to retrieve assets.

input_parameters.name: assetType
description: Optional. A comma-separated list of asset types from which to retrieve information. Asset types include Flow Service, Java Service, Document Type, etc. To retrieve a list of all asset types, call the GET/admin/assettype operation in the Integration Server Administrator API.

input_parameters.name: visible
description: - true to retrieve assets from other packages when flagged as visible.
- false to not retrieve assets from other packages.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to specify whether or not an asset can be referenced from outside the package.

input_parameters.name: favorite
description: - true to retrieve the assets that are marked as a favorite.
- false to retrieve the assets that are not marked as a favorite.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to mark an asset as a favorite.

input_parameters.name: deprecated
description: - true to retrieve assets that are marked as deprecated.
- false to retrieve assets that are not marked as deprecated.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to mark an asset as deprecated.

input_parameters.name: displayName
description: Optional. Local name for the asset for which to retrieve information.

input_parameters.name: since
description: Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to specify the version of the package.

input_parameters.name: tag
description: Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to assign tags to assets.

output_parameters.name: packageName
description: List of packages with assets that meet the input criteria.

children.name: name
description: Fully qualified name of the asset.

children.name: displayName
description: Local name for the asset.

children.name: type
description: Asset type, such as Flow Service, Java Service, or Document Type.

services.name: pub.assets:getChecksums
description: WmPublic. Retrieves the checksums for assets, specifically namespace elements, on Integration Server. You can calculate the checksum for specific assets or all of the assets in a package. A checksum is a digest for an asset that is generated using all the properties and contents of the asset. Each asset on Integration Server has a unique checksum. Two assets on different Integration Servers have the same checksum if the assets are identical.

input_parameters.name: packages
description: You must specify a value for packages or assets. If you specify a value for both packages and assets, Integration Server uses the packages value and ignores assets. If you specify neither, the pub.assets:getChecksums service ends with an exception.

input_parameters.name: assets
description: You must specify a value for packages or assets. If you specify a value for both packages and assets, Integration Server uses the packages value and ignores assets. If you specify neither, the pub.assets:getChecksums service ends with an exception.

output_parameters.name: checksums
description: List of checksums for the assets in the specified packages or the specified assets.

children.name: package
description: Name of the package containing the asset.

children.name: name
description: Fully qualified name of the asset.

children.name: checksum
description: The checksum value is null for an asset listed in assets if the specified asset does not exist on Integration Server.

usage_notes: The pub.assets:getChecksums service ends with an exception if a package identified in packages does not exist or is not enabled.
The checksums/checksum output parameter contains a null value if an asset specified in assets does not exist.
If you do not have List access to a particular asset, the service returns only the asset name. The package and checksum values for the particular asset are null.

You can only compare packages and assets from the same version of Integration Server. Comparison of assets across different versions of Integration Server is not supported. Internal changes in assets from one release to another might result in functionally identical assets being reported as different.

services.name: pub.assets:getGlobalVariableWithDependents
description: WmPublic. Retrieves the global variables used in the package along with a list of assets that depend on (use) the global variable.

input_parameters.name: packageName
description: Name of the package from which to retrieve global variable usage information.

input_parameters.name: keys
description: Optional. A list of global variables from which you want to retrieve information. If keys are not provided, the service returns all the global variables used in the package.

output_parameters.name: result
description: The list of global variables in a package.

children.name: key
description: Name of the global variable.

children.name: value
description: Value of the global variable.

children.name: isSecure
description: - true indicates that the global variable value is handled in a secure manner.
- false indicates that the global variable value is not handled in a secure manner.

children.name: source
description: For a server-level global variable, returns server.

children.name: dependants
description: A list of assets that depend on or use this global variable.

services.name: pub.assets:getHostedAPIs
description: WmPublic. Retrieves the list of APIs in a package on Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: packageList
description: A package or a list of packages for which you want to view APIs.

output_parameters.name: packages
description: The list of packages that contains APIs.

children.name: packageName
description: Name of a package.

children.name: api
description: The list of all APIs in a package.

children.name: apiName
description: The name of an API.

children.name: type
description: Supported types are WSDL, SWAGGER, and OPENAPI.

children.name: endpointUrl
description: Note: The service does not retrieve the endpointUrl for a consumer REST API or REST API descriptor.

services.name: pub.assets:getPackageAssets
description: WmPublic. Retrieves a list of assets in a package, optionally limiting results to a type of asset.

input_parameters.name: packageName
description: Name of the package from which to retrieve asset information.

input_parameters.name: assetType
description: Optional. A list of asset types you want to retrieve information. Asset types include Flow Service, Java Service, Document Type, etc.

input_parameters.name: includeDependency
description: - true to generate a list of dependencies or references for each asset.

- false to skip generating the list of dependents and references assets. The dependents, references, dependentsError, and referencesError output parameters are not returned. This is the default setting.

output_parameters.name: asset
description: Asset information for the package.

children.name: name
description: Fully qualified name of the asset.

children.name: type
description: Asset type, such as Flow Service, Java Service, or Document Type.

children.name: displayName
description: Local name for the asset.

children.name: category
description: Asset category, such as Service, Document, or Trigger.

children.name: visible
description: - private indicates that the asset cannot be referenced from outside the package.
- public indicates that the asset can be referenced from outside the package.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to specify whether or not an asset can be referenced from outside the package.

children.name: favorite
description: - true indicates that the asset is marked as a favorite.
- false indicates that the asset is not marked as a favorite.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to mark an asset as a favorite.

children.name: deprecated
description: - true indicates that the asset is deprecated.
- false indicates that the asset is not deprecated.
Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to mark an asset as deprecated.

children.name: since
description: Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to specify the version of the package.

children.name: tag
description: Note: Use IBM webMethods.io Integration to assign tags to assets.

children.name: dependents
description: A list of assets that depend on or use this asset. If there are no dependents, or there are errors retrieving dependents, the service returns an empty list.

children.name: references
description: A list of assets that are referenced or used by this asset. If there are no references, or there are errors retrieving references, the service returns an empty list.

children.name: dependentsError
description: Conditional. Message indicating the error preventing retrieval of a list of dependent assets. This parameter is returned only if there is an error in retrieving the dependent assets.

children.name: referencesError
description: Conditional. Message indicating the error preventing to retrieve a list of referenced assets. This parameter is returned only if there is any error in retrieving the referenced assets.
